#1809 — Migrate yonatan-hq/platform → orchestkit-hook-contract M141-8

Before / after design · verified at HEAD (platform feat/8165-transcripts-cutover, PyPI v0.1.1) · 2026-07-14
🛑
The issue asks for a byte-compatible drop-in swap — "remove local model, import from package, no behavior changes." Verified against live source, that is not achievable: the package (minimal, portable) and the platform (analytics-enriched ingest) have diverged on all three axes the swap touches. A naive swap silently drops data and 401s live traffic. The realistic reframe is below.

1 Ingest flow — pick a state

Current platform ingest — legacy sha256= HMAC, 58-event enum, analytics envelope.

2 The three hard incompatibilities

Axis 1 · Enum

🔢 58 ⊋ 19 events

before58 members +_missing_→GENERIC
after19 members strict union
✗ deletes 39 live event types
(McpToolCall, Ork*×12, hq_*×5, TaskCreate, AgentSpawn…) + loses unknown-event fallback
Axis 2 · Payload

📦 envelope ⊋ minimal

before+~15 fields data/metadata:Any
afterper-event no analytics
✗ drops trace_id, cost_usd, tokens, tool/error counts…
breaks t1/t2 extractors + redis_stats (rely on dict[str,Any])
Axis 3 · Signature

🔐 wire mismatch

beforesha256=/sha512= · no replay
aftert=,v1= · 300s replay
✗ 401s ALL currently-signed traffic
every emitter must cut over simultaneously

Python floor (platform ≥3.13 vs package ≥3.11) is the only clean axis — compatible. Blast radius if forced: ~15 hot-path modules + ~40 analytics importers via the cc_hooks/__init__ re-export.

3 Real-world examples

▶🔐 Signature header — legacy vs package (the same X-CC-Hooks-Signature header, two formats) 
# BEFORE — platform core/security.py verify_webhook_signature()
X-CC-Hooks-Signature: sha256=4c2f9a1b…e90d   # HMAC-SHA256(body), no timestamp
X-CC-Hooks-Signature: sha512=8813f7…22ab   # or SHA-512, still no replay window

# AFTER — package sign()/verify(): t=<unix>,v1=<hex>
X-CC-Hooks-Signature: t=1731000000,v1=4c2f…e9  # HMAC-SHA256("<ts>." + body), 300s replay tolerance
# ▲ signed payload = timestamp + "." + rawBody  →  stale request (>300s) is rejected reason=stale
▶📨 Ingest payload — real platform fixture (tests/fixtures/cc_hooks.py) 
{
  "event": "UserPromptSubmit",          # CCHookEvent — 1 of platform's 58
  "session_id": "sess-001",
  "project": "yonatan-hq",
  "timestamp": "2026-02-28T00:00:00Z",
  "data": { "prompt": "hello" },        # dict[str,Any] — t1/t2 extractors read this
  "metadata": { "branch": "dev", "model": "opus" }
  # + trace_id, user_id, cost_usd, total_tokens, tool_count, error_count … (all analytics-only)
}
# The package's per-event UserPromptSubmitPayload has NONE of the analytics fields → drop-in loses them.
▶✅ Option A — superset parity gate (new CI test in platform) 
from orchestkit_hook_contract import HOOK_EVENT_NAMES, PAYLOAD_SCHEMAS
from app.schemas.cc_hooks import CCHookEvent, HookPayload

def test_platform_is_superset_of_contract():
    # every package event MUST exist in platform's enum — dependabot bump fails here until added
    platform = {e.value for e in CCHookEvent}
    assert set(HOOK_EVENT_NAMES) <= platform, set(HOOK_EVENT_NAMES) - platform

def test_contract_schema_validates_platform_payload():
    # package JSON Schema must accept platform's serialized payload for shared events
    p = HookPayload(event="UserPromptSubmit", session_id="s", project="p", timestamp=NOW)
    jsonschema.validate(p.model_dump(mode="json"), PAYLOAD_SCHEMAS["UserPromptSubmit"])
▶✅ Option A — dual-accept signature (mirrors existing test_cc_hooks_dual_token_accept.py) 
from orchestkit_hook_contract import verify as verify_v1   # t=,v1= format

def verify_hook_signature(body, header, secret):
    # NEW: accept the package's replay-safe format …
    if header and header.startswith("t="):
        return verify_v1(header, body, secret).valid
    # … while still accepting legacy sha256=/sha512= during the #1861 channel-4 cutover
    return verify_webhook_signature(body, header, secret)
# ▲ zero live traffic breaks; emitters migrate at their own pace, gate flips when channel-4 canonical

4 Scope options

OptionWhat ships in platformDiff shapeRisk
A · Conformance + dual-accept add dep · superset parity CI gate · dual-accept signatures (sha256= and t=,v1=) additive — 0 model deletions low RECOMMENDED
C · Defer, parity-only add dep · parity CI gate only; signature dual-accept deferred to #1861 smallest lowest
B · Expand package to superset add 39 events + analytics envelope to package, republish, then swap inverts dep direction high defeats "portable" claim
Option A end state — both signature formats accepted, platform stays the superset:

5 Changeover — what changes in each repo

🟣 orchestkit (source of truth)

publishes the contract

#1806packages/hook-contract/openapi/sink.yaml — OpenAPI 3.1 ✅ shipped
#1808examples/generic-client/ — portable example + 5 tests ✅ shipped
flowspec/hook-events.spec.yml → codegen → npm + PyPI publish
noteAdding an event = edit spec, bump, publish. No platform edit here.
🔵 platform (consumer · #1809 = Option A)

tracks + validates it

depapps/api/pyproject.toml +orchestkit-hook-contract>=0.1.1
gatetests/…/test_contract_parity.py — 19 ⊆ 58 + schema-validates (NEW)
sigcore/security.py — dual-accept t=,v1= OR sha256=
keepLocal CCHookEvent(58) + HookPayload stay — package is validated against, not swapped in.

Net: orchestkit already shipped its half (#1806/#1808). Platform's half is additive — a dep, a parity test, and a dual-accept branch. Zero model deletions, zero live-traffic breakage. A future event added in orchestkit's spec flows to platform via dependabot and fails the parity gate until platform's enum catches up — drift becomes impossible to merge silently.

Full markdown design at scratchpad/1809-platform-migration-design.md. Implementation lands in Yonatan-HQ/platform (separate repo) — presented before/after first; implement after sign-off. Recommendation: Option A — the only version achievable at HEAD, non-breaking to live ingest, and it actually proves the export contract end-to-end.
